refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall derive performance requirements from the worksite mission context.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR combined multiple obligations: deriving, recording, and managing performance requirements. To ensure atomicity and solution neutrality, these functions are separated. This refined FR focuses solely on the derivation of performance requirements, which is a distinct functional need independent of how the requirements are recorded or managed.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify that the system can analyze worksite mission context inputs and produce a documented set of performance requirements such as cycle time, energy efficiency, and availability without specifying how these are stored or managed.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall record performance requirements derived from the worksite mission context.
The NGHE solution shall manage performance requirements derived from the worksite mission context.

candidate_dps: Define a logical model to translate worksite mission context into performance requirement parameters
Establish a traceable mapping framework linking worksite inputs to performance targets
Implement a version-controlled repository to document and maintain performance baselines and calibration updates
Incorporate safety and operational policy constraints into performance requirement derivation
selected_dp.selected_dp: Define a logical model to translate worksite mission context into performance requirement parameters

potential_sources: Tight integration between worksite input processing and performance target generation
Dependency on safety and operational policies for requirement constraints
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on a logical model that abstracts the translation process, minimizing direct coupling to specific data sources or policies. Other candidate DPs address traceability and documentation, which can be modularized separately to maintain independence.

assumptions_risks: Assumes worksite mission context inputs are well-defined and accessible
Risk that safety and operational policies may change frequently, requiring adaptable model updates
Assumes version control and documentation mechanisms are implemented in separate modules to avoid coupling
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly separating traceability and documentation functions into distinct DPs to enhance modularity and maintain clear boundaries between requirement derivation and record-keeping.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the core logic and processing capabilities to translate worksite mission context into performance requirement parameters, serving as the central system intelligence.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives real-time data about the worksite and vehicle status to inform the logical model.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives mission context and task assignments from higher-level control or operator inputs.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Sends alerts if performance parameters indicate safety concerns.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Facilitates communication with other modules and external systems for data exchange and coordination.

module_containment_scores.notes: Highly cohesive as the logical model is central to NGHE Solution; minimal diffusion as most data inputs and outputs are handled internally or via defined interfaces.
Vehicle module is more about physical control and actuation; containing the logical model here would cause significant diffusion to other modules for mission context and safety.
Teleoperation focuses on remote control and command; embedding the logical model here would diffuse mission context processing outside its primary scope.
Safety Services could use performance parameters but is not the best place to contain the entire logical model; would cause moderate diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate and specific boundary to contain the logical model translating mission context into performance parameters, as it centralizes core system intelligence and minimizes diffusion across modules.
containment_risks: Potential complexity growth in NGHE Solution if the logical model expands significantly, risking module bloat.
Dependency on accurate and timely data from external interfaces may affect model performance and reliability.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP logically addresses the FR by defining a model that translates the worksite mission context into performance requirement parameters, directly fulfilling the requirement to derive performance requirements from mission context.

diffusion_risks: Low diffusion risk as the logical model is contained within the NGHE Solution module, which centralizes core processing and minimizes scattering across modules.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module has high containment (score 9) and low diffusion (score 2), indicating strong cohesion and appropriate architectural packaging for the DP. Other modules show higher diffusion scores, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the IM boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through documented traceability of performance targets to worksite inputs and maintaining history of baselines/calibration updates as per acceptance criteria.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution IM is clear and well-justified, with defined interfaces supporting data inputs and outputs necessary for the function.

recommended_revisions: Monitor complexity growth within the NGHE Solution module to avoid module bloat as the logical model expands.
Ensure robust handling and validation of data from external interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, Network Link) to maintain model performance and reliability.
Document interface contracts and data flow explicitly to support verification and future maintenance.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with strong justification, low diffusion risk, and clear allocation, indicating a sound current-level decomposition. Given the high safety criticality, further decomposition of the FR into next-level sub-FRs is prudent to ensure detailed traceability, verification, and risk mitigation at finer granularity. This will support managing complexity growth and robust interface handling as recommended in the revisions.
next_level_sub_frs: Derive specific performance parameters from mission context inputs
Validate and calibrate performance requirement parameters
Manage interface data flows from Telemetry, Tasking, and Network Link

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module will maintain high cohesion as complexity grows
Interfaces from external systems are stable and well-defined
open_risks: Potential module bloat in NGHE Solution as logical model expands
Data validation failures from external interfaces impacting model accuracy
evidence_needed: Detailed interface contracts and data flow documentation
Verification records linking performance targets to worksite inputs and calibration history
